text: Three-layer data flow traced: (1) Array layer: `_wrapreduction` in `numpy/_core/fromnumeric.py` (line 66) is the canonical Python entry point for array-level aggregation — it dispatches `np.sum`, `np.prod`, `np.mean`, etc. by calling `ufunc.reduce()` on raw ndarray objects, which bottoms out at `PyUFunc_Reduce` in `ufunc_object.c`. (2) Data structure layer: `NumpyExtensionArray` in `pandas/core/arrays/numpy_.py` (line 61) wraps a NumPy ndarray as a pandas ExtensionArray, bridging NumPy storage with the pandas type system. (3) Scientific computation layer: `describe()` in `scipy/stats/_stats_py.py` (line 1444) accepts array_like inputs (numpy arrays or pandas Series) and computes descriptive statistics (nobs, min/max, mean, variance, skewness, kurtosis), internally using numpy aggregation functions like `xp.min`, `xp.max`, `xp.mean`.
